sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.38 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: (J.\,G.\,Thompson). For a finite group$G$, we denote by$N(G)$the set of all orders of the conjugacy classes of$G$. Is it true that if$G$is a finite non-abelian simple group,$H$a finite group with trivial centre and$N(G)=N(H)$, then$G$and$H$are isomorphic? { A.\,S.\,Kondratiev, W.\,J.\,Shi} Yes, it is true. The final step of the proof is in the paper (I.\,B.\,Gorshkov, Commun. Algebra, { 47}, no.\,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
